word.item: place name, comprises the pine-covered land extending from Alexandria to Lake Charles
I might know, I probably know
I roast over fire on spit
you (2 m.) already loaned me money

morph.item: a non grammatical form for 2FD perfect tense

word.item: you (2 f.) already loaned me money

morph.item: ungrammatical form of 1S class II completive

word.item: I'm going to loan him money
Are you (m.) going to lend me money?
Are you (f.) going to lend me money?
Is he going to lend you (m.) money
they (m. pl.) already loaned me money
they (f. pl.) already loaned me money
maybe Atakapa says S., but then he says he coud not say what tribe that was (poss. Karankawa)
maybe Sioux, says S., poss. Blackfeet
Biloxi (it says different ground, I believe, says S.)
right now I'm going to go
the fem. tiger is going to growl
how do you (m.) do to walk?
how do you (f.) do to walk?
how do you (f.) do to sing?
how do you (m.) do to play?
he's going to crawl (like a snake)
he sharpened the stick to a point
actually said by S. first for form in middle of p.
S. doesn't know what this means
wreath, crown-like oranament of metal (often gold)
I catch it and hold it
he's going to come day after tomorrow
I already jabbed him (with my elbow)
I've been in the woods till I die
he's free (lit. they set him free)
she's free (lit. they set her free)
salsepareille (around Charenton; around Marksville another vine is called salsepareille)
something you stick in the ground
I stick the stick in the ground
I stick the stick in the sugar
I stick the stick in the bottle
I stand on my hands and feet
I walk on my hand and feet
I take a sip of water
a crack is open, spread apart
I peep, peek (nobody knows you see)
(a kind of lizard with a white stripe down its back)
I embrace her, take her in my arms
he's copulating (this is the right way to say it)
he's going in order to copulate

interlinear-text.item: contains years regarding start and end of notes

word.item: cane whistle (used to imitate deer in hunting)
they display a deer-tail (as part of dress in dance)
Cr. sureau dry the flowers and make a tea that is good for fever
the food was too hot, it burned me
I sealed it with boiling water
silver pieces with hole in middle used as ornaments on clothing
the sun is not quite down
good all the time, very good
A man gets acquainted with a girl
I am going break and burst it
she tickles me with her feather
I am sitting in the shade
a working basket, a sewing basket
I'm going to make it wet
Now I know how to run.
I stir up my coffee, it's sweet enough
bigger than lizard got red head, salamander
something against you 'spose you sin; you didn't fast right
he's born a cripple, he disobeyed, failed to observe religious rites, fasts, or the like
I hang it and turn it around (meat on a spit)
I stick the meat on a spit
I pull and clean (prepare) moss for a mattress
I break crosswise ( a stick, limb, etc..
I sweep the house with a broom
I carry the baby in my arms
I move with my arm, I hug
I'm fixing the sheets, spreading out the sheets
worm, he stay in rotten tree, he's black
a flat worm (don't say which kind & worm flat, white worm lays in moist place)
he's got a little flat foot
I mash it, push it down
I'm going to sharpen his kinfe
swamp bird something like crane, teal duck (de bɛ´kaląset)
she's going to bear a child
I'm wrapping the baby, putting its clothes on
Torch made by cutting cypress and tying it together in a bunch.
little plasters that we wore, small pieces of metal (round)
I'm shaking it out some cloth
I stretch when I feel tired
thin, lean (like when you get sick)
it expands, fills out {Fr. sa mu´sti}
breaking small branches off large ones
I put all the limbs together
big bird that stays in water (water turkey) de grands gosier bill
I blow my nose (sneeze and it sprays out) make a noise with a bad cold
a cane is used to tighten the weave of a basket [I'm beating, pounding (rice)]
he sings klu klu (of a turkey only) he sings in the fall
he's driving a post in the ground
a tuft, clump (a big bunch spread out on top) (S. can't explain this word in fr. or eng.)
he tripped and slipping off, he's slipping
tree sp. old Indian used to use the ashes of this tree to make hominy
name of Sam's grandfather . S's father father
thrush, oiseau des carnes, de zozo də kan
to make a noise, it's going to be a noise
the bell is going to ring
In the morning the mist is going to fall
the stars are falling, shooting stars
soup made with grits in it
I bump my head against the door
I'm parching corn (could use this with coffee too)
I pull it apart, tear it up
I embrace her, take her in my arms
a woman's breasts, may be applied to a man's breast
I want to come on hand, it's just coming

interlinear-text.item: Odd Tunica (or something else) on second to last line of page

word.item: he masturbates (il a coutame de faire ça)
il a kutym də dɔne Ɛ̨ puyƐ̨t
